plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to reduce risk.
Trim QQQI and CRWD to lower overall tech exposure.
Reduce SPY to address concentration and free up capital.
Buy NVDA as a strong tech name with good potential.
Add CVX for sector diversification and stability.
This plan aims for capital preservation with modest growth.
conservative.read: This conservative approach prioritizes protecting capital. Expect lower potential returns, around 1.5% per month, with significantly reduced risk of large losses.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leverage risk.
Reduce SPY to address concentration and free up capital.
Trim QQQI to balance income exposure.
Buy NVDU as a promising tech setup.
Add PLTR for its high risk/reward potential.
This plan balances growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan seeks steady growth, targeting about 2.5% per month. It aims to reduce overall risk while still participating in market upside, with a moderate potential for drawdowns.

plan: Increase SOXL to leverage semiconductor upside.
Reduce SPY to lower broad index concentration.
Sell QQQI to exit income and free up capital.
Trim SMH to rebalance semiconductor exposure.
Increase CRWD to concentrate in strong AI/Software.
Add ERX and NVDA for high-conviction, leveraged plays.
aggressive.read: This aggressive strategy targets substantial growth, around 4% per month, by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups. Be aware of amplified potential losses, leverage decay in ETFs, and the possibility of significant drawdowns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate volatility.
Sell volatile single names like CRWD and SMH.
Increase QQQI for enhanced income generation.
Reduce SPY to manage concentration risk.
Add CVX for stability and potential income.
This plan prioritizes steady income and capital preservation.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for about 1% monthly return through distributions. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and volatile assets, prioritizing stability over growth.
